tools.name: PurpleUrchin
tools.evidence_snippet: PurpleUrchin is a South African-based threat actor group that primarily abuses cloud-based CI/CD services such as GitHub, Heroku, and Togglebox to perform their cryptomining operations.

tools.name: Kinsing
tools.evidence_snippet: Kinsing was observed to exploit weakly configured PostgreSQL containers and vulnerable images to compromise Kubernetes clusters.

tools.name: WatchDog
tools.evidence_snippet: WatchDog was observed to use Steganography to mask the transfer of malware that was hosted on compromised cloud storage buckets.
8220 was observed to deploy Tsunami IRC Botnet and remove syslog on compromised hosts.

cves.evidence_snippet: CVE-2021-40438 is one of the top 10 Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) identified in exposed cloud hosts.

ttps.technique: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs
ttps.evidence_snippet: The threat actor removed syslog on compromised hosts.
The threat actor removed the syslog files on the compromised systems.
The threat actor exploited a misconfigured security group setting during a migration process to gain access to the cloud environment.
WatchDog was observed to use Steganography to mask the transfer of malware that was hosted on compromised cloud storage buckets.
Kinsing also removed the syslog files on the compromised systems.

summary: The Cloud Threat Report, Volume 7 highlights common security issues observed across cloud environments. Hard-coded credentials, weak authentication, and disabled logging are among the top concerns.
